text.title: Stakeholder Register Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Group stakeholders split into individuals on 'list people' prompts.
antipattern.root-cause: Authority is collective; individuals cannot unblock unilaterally.
antipattern.fix: Preserve groups when authority is collective; reject prompts that force individual lists.

antipattern.symptom: Attitudes classified 'Supportive' from polite written tone.
antipattern.root-cause: Polite tone is universal; not a signal of actual support.
antipattern.fix: Require attitude evidence as quoted source; reject without quote.

antipattern.symptom: Emails, phones, salaries in the in-repo register YAML.
antipattern.root-cause: Sensitive data exposed by repo access.
antipattern.fix: Move PII to separate access-controlled file; pre-commit scan for email/phone patterns in register.yaml.

antipattern.symptom: Mid-level influencer stakeholders (procurement, security review) systematically under-rated.
antipattern.root-cause: Veto power without org-chart seniority is invisible to LLMs.
antipattern.fix: Explicit prompt: 'who has a veto even if not in the org chart?'
